summary: Confirm RP2350-PiZero pinout (HDMI/HSTX, SD, USB host)
description: Verify against the Waveshare schematic/wiki: HSTX TMDS pin pairing (GPIO12-19, clock+3 data lanes), microSD interface (SPI vs SDIO) and exact pins, and the PIO-USB host D+/D- pins on USB-C port 3. Also resolve whether the board's PSRAM footprint is populated. The wiki blocks WebFetch (403), so download the schematic/datasheet manually. Blocks display and SD bring-up.

summary: Decision: HSTX native DVI, 320x240 fb hardware-doubled to 640x480
description: Display path = HSTX native TMDS encoder (pico-examples dvi_out_hstx_encoder / Adafruit PicoDVI HSTX mode), chosen over PicoDVI-PIO to free PIO for USB host. Framebuffer = 320x240 RGB565 (~154KB, fits 520KB SRAM); HSTX scans out pixel/line-doubled to 640x480. CoCo 256x192 centers in 320x240, appearing as 512x384 with blank borders. See CLAUDE.md Display plan.

summary: Fork reusable skeleton from the AMOLED port
description: Copy board-agnostic code from ~/github/coco-rp2350-waveshare-touch-amoled-18: lib/xroar_core, lib/coco_machine, src/coco_boot.cpp/h, AUTORUN.md, deploy.sh. OMIT board-specific: lib/sh8601, lib/qspi_pio, lib/hal (touch/IMU), src/hw_config.c (AMOLED SD pins), and the AMOLED-rotation parts of the blitter. src/main.cpp copied as a starting structure but display calls will be rewritten.

summary: PlatformIO config for RP2350B + earlephilhower + PicoDVI
description: platformio.ini: RP2350B board id (-DPICO_RP2350B=1), framework=arduino (earlephilhower), lib_deps Adafruit PicoDVI (HSTX), Pico-PIO-USB + Adafruit TinyUSB, carlk3/no-OS-FatFS-SD. Keep -O2, build_unflags=-Os, lib_ldf_mode=deep+. Arduino.h before XRoar headers (_Bool gotcha).

summary: HSTX DVI bring-up: 320x240 test pattern on HDMI monitor
description: Initialize HSTX DVI at 640x480 with a 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer (hardware pixel/line doubling). Display a static test pattern (color bars / gradient) on a real HDMI monitor. Confirm stable sync, correct colors (watch RGB565 byte order), and that doubling works as expected.

coco_machine currently leans on PSRAM for the 64KB CoCo RAM. PiZero PSRAM may be unpopulated; allocate CoCo RAM from main SRAM (static/SRAM malloc) like the AMOLED port did. Verify against the SRAM budget alongside the 154KB framebuffer.

Re-derive the blitter for the 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer: map the nibble-packed VDG 256x192 buffer + palette into a centered 256x192 region (32px L/R, 24px T/B border), no rotation (landscape native). Re-derive any lookup tables. Simpler than the AMOLED portrait-rotation math.

summary: SD mount + ROM load on PiZero SD pins
description: Wire FatFs to the PiZero microSD pins (from PIZERO-01). Mount the card and load /coco/bas12.rom + /coco/extbas11.rom into the 16KB ROM image via the existing coco_boot ROM loader.

summary: Milestone: Color BASIC boots to OK on HDMI
description: Integrate emulator loop + blitter + DVI scanout: XRoar runs, VDG output renders, Color BASIC reaches the OK prompt on the HDMI monitor. Parity with the AMOLED port's Phase 2.

Get the AUTORUN.TXT autotype + disk/cart demo path working on HDMI (parity with AMOLED Phase 3): @DISK/@CART/@DIRECT directives, LOADM .bin direct-load, autotype queue. Confirm a self-running BASIC/demo on the monitor.

summary: PIO-USB host bring-up: enumerate a USB keyboard
description: Bring up Pico-PIO-USB + Adafruit TinyUSB host on USB-C port 3 (pins from PIZERO-01), reusing the pattern from ~/github/waveshare-rp2350-usb-a. Enumerate a plugged-in USB keyboard and log HID reports over serial. Mind the PIO-USB clock requirement.

summary: Map USB HID keycodes -> CoCo keyboard matrix
description: Translate USB HID keyboard reports to CoCo DSCAN codes and inject via the existing coco_machine_press_key() matrix hook. Handle modifiers/shift and key-hold/debounce (the AMOLED port held keys a few frames). Replaces serial/autotype as the primary input.
Map a USB HID gamepad to the CoCo joystick axes/buttons through the XRoar joystick path. Stretch goal after keyboard works.

summary: Dual-core split: emu on core0, video/USB/SD on core1
description: core0 = CoCo emulation; core1 = blit + DVI scanout + SD/USB servicing (mirrors AMOLED core split, adds USB host). Reconcile the PIO-USB host clock requirement (USB-A repo notes ~120MHz) against emulation pacing (AMOLED used 250MHz). Target >=30fps per the README.

summary: Performance pass: instrument timing, tune f_cpu/vreg
description: Add per-frame timing instrumentation (CPU/blit/scanout) like the AMOLED port. Tune f_cpu and vreg within USB-PIO timing constraints to hit >=30fps. Compare real-time % against the AMOLED port baseline.
